name: Notes from a Soothsayer
author: Jeraldine Haverlowe
description:
Notes from a Soothsayer — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Jeraldine Haverlowe is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1492 DR.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.

name: Nether Scrolls (book)
author: Unknown
description:
Nether Scrolls (book) — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.

name: Night-Night, Pirate Ship
author: Unknown
description:
Night-Night, Pirate Ship — A popular tale in print — performed in taverns before it was ever bound between covers. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1492 DR.

Critics call it low art; innkeepers call it excellent for business on rainy nights.

name: Nine Stars Around a Silver Moon
author: Mintiper Moonsilver
description:
Nine Stars Around a Silver Moon — Legends collected from tavern verse, elder memory, and sources no scholar will swear to in court. Mintiper Moonsilver is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1336 DR.

Each generation adds a verse; purists claim the oldest layer is true and everything since is flattery.
